content:
- started choose-task turn
- read list-active-tasks skill and attempted progress read; no prior progress content was returned
- refreshing Ralph task summary next, then reading current_tasks/current_tasks_done to resolve blockers and select the next task

- refreshed Ralph summaries and inspected active candidate tasks plus completion ledger
- found two active failing tasks; the Cachix workflow bug is unblocked, marked <priority>high</priority>, and outranks the docs task by the mandatory bug-first rule
- confirmed the repository is a Nix-driven Rust workspace with a live .github/workflows/publish-images.yml pipeline using the setup-nix-cachix action; next step is to set .ralph/current_task.txt to the bug task path and quit

- read previous progress, current task pointer, and required improve-code-boundaries/tdd skill instructions
- confirmed the active task is .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-investigate-transient-cachix-502-push-failures-in-publish-images-workflow.md
- reading the task file and repo state next to determine whether this is a planning pass or an execution pass

- determined the active bug task is still pre-execution, so this turn must produce a plan file and stop
- inspected repository plan layout conventions to match the expected *_plans directory and dated filename pattern
- reading the publish-images workflow and improve-code-boundaries skill directory now so the plan can target the real failure surface and any boundary cleanup opportunity

- read the active Cachix 502 workflow bug task and confirmed it was still in the planning state with no plan pointer
- inspected the current publish-images workflow, the local setup-nix-cachix composite action, the improve-code-boundaries smell guidance, and adjacent Cachix migration plan context
- created .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-investigate-transient-cachix-502-push-failures-in-publish-images-workflow_plans/2026-04-28-cachix-502-push-failure-investigation-plan.md with execution slices centered on hosted reproduction, exact store-path cache verification, root-cause assignment, and a boundary-driven fix only if repo-side action is justified
- updated the bug task file to point at the new plan and left the plan ending at TO BE VERIFIED as required
- next turn should review the plan, switch it to NOW EXECUTE only once the investigation design is considered solid, and then quit immediately per the Ralph flow

- read prior progress, current task pointer, and required tdd/improve-code-boundaries skill instructions
- confirmed the active task remains the Cachix 502 publish-images workflow bug and is likely still in planning/verification state
- reading the task file and referenced plan now to decide whether this turn must only switch plan state or begin execution

- verified the Cachix 502 investigation plan against the real publish-images workflow and the local setup-nix-cachix composite action
- confirmed the plan's boundary choice is sound: setup action stays narrow, hosted evidence drives whether any repo-owned cache-health reporting/policy step is needed
- switched .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-investigate-transient-cachix-502-push-failures-in-publish-images-workflow_plans/2026-04-28-cachix-502-push-failure-investigation-plan.md from TO BE VERIFIED to NOW EXECUTE
- next turn should read the plan in NOW EXECUTE state, perform the hosted investigation/fix flow, run make check/lint/test if code changes occur, and continue until the task fully passes

- loaded prior progress, current task pointer, and required tdd / improve-code-boundaries skill instructions
- confirmed the active task is the Cachix 502 publish-images workflow bug and prior progress says the plan is already in NOW EXECUTE state
- reading the task file, referenced plan, and skill directory contents now so execution follows the written plan and final task checklist exactly

- read the active bug task and its referenced plan; confirmed the plan ends in NOW EXECUTE so this turn must execute rather than re-plan
- read the full improve-code-boundaries smell directory to keep the cache bootstrap boundary narrow and avoid hiding policy/reporting inside the setup helper
- next I am reading the GitHub API auth wrapper skill, checking git/HEAD/workflow state, and then moving into hosted evidence collection for the Cachix 502 failure

- read github-api-auth-wrapper guidance and am using /home/joshazimullah.linux/github-api-curl rather than exposing any token
- inspected the live workflow and setup composite action; current boundary is still narrow: publish-images.yml orchestrates jobs and setup-nix-cachix/action.yml only installs Nix and enables Cachix
- moving into hosted evidence collection now by querying the prior run and recent push-triggered publish-images workflow runs on master

- discovered there is already a fresh push-triggered Publish Images run on current HEAD: 25078705823, so execution can use that as the present-tense reproduction target
- querying the prior hosted run 25077991160 now for job metadata and downloading its authenticated logs archive to extract exact Cachix post-step 502 evidence
- after that I will compare the fresh HEAD run against the same failure signature rather than guessing from local workflow code

- prior run 25077991160 hosted logs are downloaded locally and already show real 502 Bad Gateway failures in the Cachix multipart-nar POST during post-run uploads
- the workflow itself still succeeded, which confirms the bug is specifically hidden cache-upload degradation rather than a job failure in publish logic
- I am reducing the prior logs into exact per-job failure counts and failed store paths, while also polling the fresh HEAD run 25078705823 for completion

- reduced the old run to 100 unique failed store-path uploads across three jobs: nix flake check, build runner-image amd64, and build runner-image arm64
- next step is concrete cache verification against https://djosh34.cachix.org for those exact failed /nix/store paths to determine whether the failures left real cache gaps
- the fresh HEAD run 25078705823 is still in progress, so I am using that time to settle the old-run cache completeness question

- sampled the old failed-path set against the public Cachix store and confirmed at least one path marked "Failed to push" is actually present later, so raw failure lines overstate the final damage
- now running a full presence scan across all 100 unique failed paths to quantify which uploads are eventually available and which remain absent
- also polling the fresh HEAD run jobs so I can compare the current behavior as soon as that run finishes

- official cachix-action v17 docs confirm the action is centered on daemon/store-scan push modes and do not expose a workflow-level "fail on some upload errors" control
- combined with the Cloudflare response body in the hosted logs, current evidence points more toward service-side upload availability plus weak explicit reporting than toward bad repo configuration
- waiting on the full failed-path presence scan and the fresh HEAD run completion to decide whether any repo-side policy/reporting step is justified

- full old-run cache verification is much better than expected: 99 of the 100 failed upload paths are present in djosh34.cachix.org after the run
- the only remaining miss is /nix/store/sax580snmakhf2qb5s79k90bn1v9rsyn-cargo-src-whoami-1.6.1, which I am now checking against cache.nixos.org because Cachix intentionally skips duplicating upstream-available paths
- if that path is upstream-available, the old-run completeness issue is effectively zero despite the noisy 502 post-step logs

- 99/100 old failed paths are present in djosh34.cachix.org; the only remaining miss is a cargo-src path, not an obvious final artifact output
- because that distinction matters, I am now tracing the actual image build outputs from the hosted logs to verify whether the important package outputs are present even if one source path never landed in the project cache
- I am also continuing to poll the fresh HEAD run for completion so I can confirm whether the 502 pattern still reproduces on current code

- extracted the concrete top-level image and package output store paths from the old hosted logs; the post-step explicitly logged successful pushes for the four docker image tarballs and related config/layer outputs
- verifying those top-level outputs directly in djosh34.cachix.org now to distinguish a non-critical source-path miss from a real consumer-facing cache gap
- still polling the fresh HEAD run so I can answer whether the 502 pattern is a one-off outage or still actively reproducing

- old-run evidence now shows the consumer-facing outputs are in cache: both runner-image tarballs, both verify-image tarballs, and the runner/verify package outputs all resolve from djosh34.cachix.org
- that leaves the bug looking like upstream/service-side multipart upload degradation with one observed non-artifact cargo-src miss, not a broken publish workflow artifact boundary
- running mandatory local validation now while continuing to poll the fresh HEAD run for whether the 502 signature still reproduces on current code

- instead of waiting for the whole current workflow, I am using per-job log downloads for completed jobs in run 25078705823 so I can test the 502 signature as soon as each relevant job finishes
- first target is build runner-image (arm64), because that job already completed and has the same Cachix post-step surface that showed failures in the old run
- make check is also running in parallel to satisfy the required repo validation lane

- current HEAD run 25078705823 already differs from the bad run in one key slice: build runner-image (arm64) completed with zero exact Cachix 502/failure markers, and its layer/tarball pushed cleanly
- still waiting on the current build runner-image (amd64) and nix flake check jobs, because those were two of the three jobs that emitted 502 upload noise in the old run
- make check remains in flight in parallel

- per-job inspection of the fresh HEAD run shows build runner-image (arm64) completed with zero exact Cachix 502/failure markers and clean pushes for its top-level layer and tarball
- still waiting for build runner-image (amd64) and nix flake check in that same run, because those were the remaining bad jobs in the old run and will decide whether the bug still reproduces today
- after hosted evidence settles I will rerun the mandatory local validation commands cleanly and then update the task file with the conclusion

- reran make check explicitly and it exited cleanly
- make lint and make test are running now to satisfy the remaining mandatory local validation gates while the hosted workflow continues toward completion
- once those complete I only need the final hosted conclusion written back into the task file before closing the task if no repo-side fix is justified

- make lint exited cleanly
- make test exited cleanly
- only the final hosted run conclusion remains before I can update the task file and close the bug as either resolved/transient or still reproducing

- the workflow status endpoint is still waiting on build runner-image (amd64) and nix flake check, so I am inspecting those in-progress job logs directly rather than guessing
- this will show whether the current run is already emitting the old exact 502/Failed-to-push signature before the overall run reaches completed state
- local validation is done cleanly; only hosted evidence remains

- current HEAD run now has build runner-image (amd64) completed successfully; I am fetching its final log archive immediately to check the exact Cachix post-step outcome
- if that job is clean too, only nix flake check remains as the last relevant current-run comparison point against the old 502-heavy run
- local validation is already complete and passing

- current HEAD run evidence is now strong for both runner-image jobs: amd64 and arm64 completed with zero exact 502/failure markers, and both top-level docker image tarballs plus layers pushed successfully
- the only remaining hosted comparison point is nix flake check, because that was the third noisy job in the old run
- once nix flake check completes I should have enough hosted evidence to conclude the bug no longer reproduces on current HEAD and is best explained as transient Cachix service-side availability degradation

- nix flake check is still the only unfinished job in the fresh HEAD run, and GitHub's per-job log endpoint currently returns BlobNotFound for that in-progress job
- that suggests the log blob is not finalized yet rather than giving any new evidence of Cachix failure
- waiting for that one job to finish is now the last blocker before I can update the task markdown and close the task

- inspected the current nix flake check job metadata directly; it is still inside step 4 (Run nix flake check) and has not yet entered the Cachix post-step where the old run emitted 502 noise
- that means the remaining delay is ordinary build/test time, not evidence of a post-step hang or fresh Cachix failure yet
- waiting for the step transition is the last meaningful action before task-file updates

- both current runner-image jobs are conclusively clean and local validation is green
- only current nix flake check remains, and it is still genuinely inside the main flake-check step rather than in the Cachix post-step
- giving it another interval to complete before updating the task file

- the fresh HEAD run finally completed nix flake check, so I am fetching its final log archive now for the last exact 502/failure-marker comparison against the old run
- if nix flake check is clean too, the hosted evidence says the bug no longer reproduces on current HEAD and the old failure was transient service-side availability degradation rather than repo misconfiguration
- next step after this fetch is updating the task markdown and closing it with the hosted evidence summary

- updated the bug task markdown to completed/pass with hosted evidence, cache-path verification summary, and local validation results
- next I am running .ralph/task_switch.sh as required, then staging all changes, committing with the task-finished message, and pushing
- no repo-side code or workflow changes were justified; the conclusion is transient upstream/service-side Cachix availability degradation that no longer reproduces on current HEAD
